learning_outcomes.description: Explain advanced knowledge of the field of study, informed by current research, theory, and scholarly debate.
Propose justified research problems within the field and rigorous research questions and/or hypotheses.
Conduct a substantial research project by applying appropriate methodologies in a systematic, critical, and evidence-based manner.
Evaluate appropriate analysis techniques and software to analyse research data, synthesising findings to draw coherent and well-justified conclusions.
Communicate research rationale, methods, and outcomes at an advanced scholarly level using discipline-appropriate conventions and technologies.
Address social and ethical considerations throughout the research process by applying relevant ethical standards.

assessments.due_date:
28/10/2026

The proposal presentation will most likely take place on Wednesday or Thursday of Teaching Week 13 in your enrolling semester. The exact date and time will be communicated by the Honours Admin team. Please also refer to the important dates listed on the Honours Community Site on Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
This research presentation provides you with an opportunity to present your research question, literature review, and proposed methodology in a small and supportive forum comprising academic staff and fellow students. You will have 15 minutes to present your progress to date at a small in-house event held at the end of this Semester, followed by 5-10 minutes for questions from the panel of assessors and the audience. Further details regarding the exact date will be provided in due course.

An assessor will be assigned and detailed feedback will be provided to both you and your supervisor to assist with the progression of your thesis.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The empirical honours thesis follows the same basic format as an empirical masters thesis or PhD thesis. To that end, unless otherwise instructed by the supervisor, the empirical honours thesis will have a minimum chapter content likely to include:

  1. Introduction
  2. Literature review
  3. Model and hypotheses
  4. Methodology of data collection
  5. Analysis, results and discussion
  6. Conclusion

The above structure is an example for an empirical thesis. In cases where a theoretical thesis is undertaken, a different structure would be required. Students are advised to seek direction of their supervisor on the appropriate structure in such cases.

Additional information related to the format, word count, ethics requirements and submission details can be found on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The thesis is to be submitted electronically through Turnitin located in the Blackboard Assessment link.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
24/05/2027 1:00 pm

Five check-ins must be completed and submitted via Blackboard by Monday of Week 26, before 1:00 pm. You are responsible for working with your supervisor to schedule these check-ins across the two semesters.
assessments.task_description:
This assessment is a series of structured sessions designed to help you steadily advance your research. These check-ins let you regularly reflect on your progress, clarify your ideas, and receive formative feedback. 

Through these check-ins, you should demonstrate active engagement with your research. This may include refining your research questions, developing your literature review, planning or conducting data work, and reflecting on challenges as well as next steps.

Ideally, the five check-ins should be spaced evenly across two semesters. For example, two of the five check-ins should be completed in your enrolling semester.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You are responsible for coordinating with your supervisor to schedule check-ins across the two semesters. Before each meeting, complete the research progress check-in template (available on Blackboard), which will help you reflect on your progress, identify challenges, and structure a focused and productive discussion with your supervisor.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
